brief_summary: Three-panel taxonomy strip showing the three switch families as small force-over-travel glyphs. Left: linear (clean diagonal). Center: tactile (curve with a single bump dot). Right: clicky (tactile shape plus a sharp click spike and small sound-wave marks). Each panel has its named exemplars below.
data_sources: beginners-switch-buying-guide.mdx § 'The three families' — 'A linear switch travels in a straight line from rest to bottom-out, with no bump and no click ... A tactile switch has a small bump partway down the press ... A clicky switch is a tactile with an added mechanism that produces a sharp, audible click at the actuation point.' Exemplars listed in same section: Oil King, Gateron Yellow, MX Red (linear); Box Brown, Holy Panda, Boba U4T (tactile); MX Blue, Box White (clicky).
